<@&1291578924005986386> Today will be an important day for us all. Here is why.

There has already been increasing fears of an 'AI bubble' which has triggerred sell-offs in tech heavy stocks, including but not limited to quantum stocks.

This may or may not be a co-incidence, but whenever we seem to reach crucial support/resistance levels on SPY and certain other stocks, news suddenly comes out, big news. This happened back during tariff announcement, tariff relief and so on. Zoom out and check the chart and compare the announcement timings to see for yourself.

We are now at another crucial support on SPY.

Amidst this tech sell off, we are also seeing reports of Trump only in the last few days, talking about a $1 trillion investment from Saudi Arabia. I think this could either be a genuine attempt at turning the market bullish admist this 'sell off' (which may confirm we may be heading into a big pop/sell off) or it could genuinely be bullish news and the timing could be another co-incidence.

Ultimately - NVDA are reporting earnings today. Yes, this company is incredibly valuable and is important to the world right now - but everyone also thought this about the internet during the late 1990's, and then we saw the burst of the .com bubble and billion dollar companies literally going bankrupt/delisting.

My take on this:

If NVDA report bad earnings, are at expectations, or way under expectation, we could actually see a intense sell off. However, if NVDA happens to give a big surprise I think we will see the market move up a bit but then sell-off again. There is a lot of fear in the market at the moment.


Nonetheless, when NVDA reports earnings today it will have a massive effect on a lot of stocks. As google CEO also stated yesterday, if the bubble was to pop, no company will be immune to what we are about to see.

I'm still holding onto all my swings and will ride this out.

I predicted this massive sell off/correction in tech stocks around 2 weeks ago. I'm probably going to stick to shorting for now.

<@&1291578924005986386> Here is why I think the next **48 hours** are going to be a golden money making opportunity for $PACS.

I initially shorted it, but after doing some deep research into this company and spending a few hours - I quickly closed my short trade and swapped sides, I'm now longing this stock.

PACS collapsed from the $40 range back in August 2024 after the Hindenburg report accused the company of abusing COVID-era Medicare waivers and billing Medicare Part B for unnecessary respiratory and therapy services. A few months later PACS admitted they overstated revenue by about $15–17 million in Q1 2024 and another $46–48 million in Q2 2024. This pushed the stock into an oversold zone for almost a year and created a massive confidence hit. Now the CEO has confirmed all delayed filings will be released on Wednesday and said the company expects record revenue and strong EBITDA for the first half of 2025. 

**IF** we genuinely do see 'record' numbers this Wednesday, the stock could easily go back to what it was at before. Remember, based on last earnings, the stock was given PTs of around $30-$40 (not including the allegations).

We have also seen over $300 million dollars get invested in this company today (market cap wise) and the technicals are also looking good with a golden cross forming. Remember, this is a risky trade like every trade - but I'm guessing this goes up.

1.    Chun Kit Yu sits on the boards of both QMMM Holdings (NASDAQ: QMMM) and Skyline Builders Group Holding (NASDAQ: SKBL).

3.    Both companies are Hong-Kong based firms that are listed on NASDAQ.

And that’s not all, here is more suspicious things…

1.    QMMM and SKBL both had public offerings at the same price. $4 per share. However, QMMM did another offering during June 2025 for $0.20 per share. This offering closed on June 20th. SKBL also did another offering – and this one closed around a week or two ago.

2.    QMMM had a few days of the stock being bought up/unusual activity, and then one explosive day which was yesterday (the stock shot up an insane amount). Guess what? SKBL is being accumulated right now.

4.    Both companies have little to no insider activity. Also, QMMM and SKBL are Foreign Private Issuers, which means they are placed OUTSIDE the scope of standard U.S. insider disclosure rules. Means transparency is limited.
